Description: Side panel showing cart contents without leaving current page
Do: Open on 'Add to cart' click. Show item count + total + checkout CTA.
Don't: Navigate to full cart page on every add — breaks shopping flow
Code_Good: <aside class='fixed right-0 w-96' role='dialog' aria-label='Shopping cart'>
Code_Bad: <a href='/cart'> on every add — user bounced from browsing
A11y_Note: Focus trap in flyout. Escape to close. Return focus to trigger.
Conversion_Note: Mini cart increases conversion 15-25% vs full page redirect.

Description: Individual item row in cart with image + qty + price + remove
Do: Show product thumbnail + name + selected options + qty selector + line total + remove.
Don't: Text-only cart with no images — users confused which items they added
Code_Good:
{name}
Size: M

${price}
Code_Bad:
• {name} x{qty} ${price}
    • — no image; no options shown; no remove
A11y_Note: Remove button needs aria-label='Remove {item name}' for context.
Conversion_Note: Show options (size/color) to reduce returns from wrong variant.

Description: Input for changing item quantity in cart
Do: Use stepper (+/-) with direct input. Min 1. Update total immediately.
Don't: Allow quantity 0 — confusing. Use remove button instead.
Code_Bad: <input type='text'> with no min/max — user enters 0 or negative
A11y_Note: aria-label on input and buttons. Announce total change.
Conversion_Note: Inline quantity update (no save button) reduces friction.

Description: Order total breakdown: subtotal + shipping + tax + discount + total
Do: Show each line item. Update dynamically when coupon/shipping changes.
Don't: Show only final total with no breakdown — feels untrustworthy
Code_Good: <dl><dt>Subtotal</dt><dd>$89.00</dd><dt>Shipping</dt><dd>$5.99</dd><dt>Tax</dt><dd>$7.60</dd><dt class='font-bold'>Total</dt><dd class='font-bold'>$102.59</dd></dl>
Code_Bad:
Total: $102.59

— no breakdown; user doesn't trust the number
A11y_Note: Use <dl> for definition list. Announce total changes via aria-live.
Conversion_Note: Price transparency increases checkout completion by 20%.

Description: Coupon/discount code entry field in cart or checkout
Do: Show input + Apply button. Validate instantly. Show discount amount.
Don't: Hide promo code field — users leave site to find codes
Code_Good: -$10.00 applied ✓
Code_Bad: No promo field visible — users google '[brand] coupon code' and abandon cart
A11y_Note: Announce discount application/error via aria-live.
Conversion_Note: Visible promo field increases AOV when users have codes.

Description: All checkout steps on one scrollable page
Do: Sections: Contact → Shipping → Payment → Review. Auto-scroll to next on complete.
Don't: Separate page for each step — too many page loads; feels slow
Code_Good: <section id='contact'>...</section><section id='shipping'>...</section><section id='payment'>...</section>
Code_Bad: Multi-page checkout with 5 separate URLs — 30% abandonment per page
A11y_Note: Focus first input of each section. Progress indicator at top.
Conversion_Note: Single page checkout converts 20-30% higher than multi-page.

Description: Allow purchase without creating an account
Do: Offer guest checkout prominently. 'Create account?' at confirmation only.
Don't: Require account creation before checkout — #1 cart abandonment reason
Code_Good: or sign in
Code_Bad:
You must create an account to continue.

— 35% abandon here
A11y_Note: Guest checkout button is naturally accessible.
Conversion_Note: Guest checkout reduces abandonment by 35%. Offer account post-purchase.

Description: Google Places / address lookup for shipping/billing address
Do: Use Places API for autocomplete. Pre-fill city/state/zip from selection.
Don't: Manual entry for all 6 address fields — slow and error-prone
Code_Good: <input type='text' id='address' autocomplete='street-address'/> + Places API suggestion dropdown
Code_Bad: 6 separate fields with no autocomplete — user types everything manually
A11y_Note: Autocomplete list as role='listbox'. Selected address fills all fields.
Conversion_Note: Address autocomplete reduces form time by 40% and errors by 50%.

Description: Radio buttons for choosing shipping speed/carrier
Do: Show method name + delivery estimate + price. Pre-select cheapest.
Don't: No delivery estimate — user picks blindly
Code_Good: <fieldset><legend>Shipping method</legend><label><input type='radio' name='shipping' checked/>Standard (5-7 days) — Free</label><label><input type='radio' name='shipping'/>Express (2-3 days) — $12.99</label></fieldset>
Code_Bad: <select><option>Standard</option><option>Express</option></select> — no delivery estimates or prices
A11y_Note: fieldset + legend for grouping. Radio buttons keyboard navigable.
Conversion_Note: Show delivery date estimate not just 'days' — 'Arrives by March 20'.

Description: Card number + expiry + CVV input fields
Do: Use single-line card input (Stripe Elements style). Format as user types.
Don't: 4 separate unformatted inputs — hard to verify card number
Code_Good: <div id='card-element'/> <!-- Stripe/Braintree hosted field --> + format: 4242 4242 4242 4242
Code_Bad: <input type='text' placeholder='Card number'> — no formatting; PCI risk
A11y_Note: Hosted payment fields handle a11y. Label the container.
Conversion_Note: NEVER handle raw card numbers. Use Stripe/Braintree hosted fields.

Description: Choose between card + PayPal + Apple Pay + Google Pay + BNPL
Do: Show digital wallets first (Apple Pay/Google Pay) — fewest steps. Then card.
Don't: Card-only payment — miss 30% who prefer digital wallets
Code_Bad: <form>Card number: <input/></form> — no alternative payment methods
A11y_Note: Each payment button needs descriptive aria-label.
Conversion_Note: Apple Pay/Google Pay convert 2x higher than manual card entry.

Description: Returning user selects from saved cards/methods
Do: Show last 4 digits + card brand icon + expiry. 'Add new' option.
Don't: Show full card numbers — security risk
Code_Good: <label><input type='radio' name='payment'/> Visa ····4242 (exp 03/27)</label>
Code_Bad: <label><input type='radio'/> 4242424242424242</label> — full number exposed
A11y_Note: Radio group with descriptive labels. Card brand icons need alt text.
Conversion_Note: Saved methods increase repeat purchase rate by 20%.

Description: Trust indicators near payment form (SSL lock + badges)
Do: Show lock icon + 'Secured by [provider]' near card input
Don't: No trust signals near payment form — user hesitates
Code_Good: 🔒 Secured by Stripe · 256-bit SSL
Code_Bad: Payment form with no security indicators — user second-guesses entering card
A11y_Note: Security text readable by screen readers. Icons have alt text.
Conversion_Note: Security badges near payment increase completion by 17%.

Description: Guarantee badge to reduce purchase anxiety
Do: Show guarantee prominently near checkout CTA: '30-day money-back guarantee'
Don't: Bury guarantee in footer or T&C — user never sees it
Code_Good: ✓ 30-day money-back guarantee · Free returns
Code_Bad: Return policy in footer — not visible at decision point
A11y_Note: Text is accessible. No a11y concern.
Conversion_Note: Visible guarantees increase conversion 12-15%.

Description: Post-purchase confirmation with order details and next steps
Do: Show order number + items + total + delivery estimate + email confirmation notice.
Don't: Redirect to homepage after purchase — user panics; no confirmation
Code_Good:
✅ Order confirmed!

Order #{id}

Confirmation sent to {email}

Estimated delivery: {date}
Code_Bad:
Thank you for your order.

— no order number; no details; no email mention
A11y_Note: Heading hierarchy. Order details in accessible format.
Conversion_Note: Confirmation page is prime real-estate for account creation prompt.

Description: Transactional email with order summary
Do: Send immediately. Include order # + items + total + tracking link + support contact.
Don't: Delayed email or no email — user contacts support thinking order failed
Code_Good: HTML email: Order #{id} | Items: {list} | Total: ${total} | Track: {link}
Code_Bad: Plain text: 'Your order is confirmed.' — no details
A11y_Note: Email must be accessible HTML with proper heading structure.
Conversion_Note: Email receipt reduces 'Where is my order?' support tickets by 40%.

Description: Overlay when user moves cursor to close/leave with items in cart
Do: Show 10% discount or free shipping offer. One popup max per session.
Don't: Show popup on every page — aggressive and annoying
Code_Good:
if(cartItems.length > 0 && exitIntent) show(
Wait! Get 10% off:
)
Code_Bad: Exit popup on every page regardless of cart — user never returns
A11y_Note: Dialog is aria-modal. Escape closes. Focus trapped inside.
Conversion_Note: Exit intent recovers 5-10% of abandoning users. One popup only.

Description: Email sent 1-24hr after cart abandonment with items + CTA
Do: Send 3-email sequence: 1hr + 24hr + 72hr. Include cart items + images + CTA.
Don't: Send 10 abandonment emails — user marks as spam
Code_Good: Email: 'You left something behind' + item images + Complete order
Code_Bad: Generic 'Come back!' email with no cart contents — user ignores
A11y_Note: Accessible HTML email. CTA button with clear text.
Conversion_Note: Abandoned cart emails recover 5-15% of abandoned revenue.

Description: Post-purchase order status tracking page
Do: Show visual timeline: Confirmed → Processing → Shipped → Delivered with dates.
Don't: Status text only with no visual progress — hard to scan
Code_Good:
1. ✓ Confirmed
  2. ✓ Shipped
  3. Delivered (est. Mar 20)
Code_Bad:
Status: Shipped

— no timeline; no estimated delivery
A11y_Note: Ordered list for timeline. Current step with aria-current='step'.
Conversion_Note: Proactive tracking reduces 'Where is my order?' support by 50%.

Description: Consistent currency display across all UI
Do: Show currency symbol + decimal always: $29.00 not $29. Use locale formatting.
Don't: Inconsistent formatting — $29 in cart but $29.00 at checkout
Code_Good: new Intl.NumberFormat('en-US', {style:'currency', currency:'USD'}).format(amount)
Code_Bad: '$' + amount — no locale; no decimal consistency
A11y_Note: Currency announced by screen readers when properly formatted.
Conversion_Note: Consistent pricing builds trust. Rounding triggers suspicion.
